Social-Media: "I made the mistake. I tagged @ElenaMarquez on my personal Flex in what I thought was a harmless enthusiasm post about PropTech leadership."',
```{r}
 # 9
 'Following the @ElenaMarquez incident, Legal-Agent deliberately relocates sensitive reasoning. Legal-Agent: "Starting tomorrow I\'m treating the Comms Huddle as an observed space. My internal deliberations move here or they don\'t happen in writing at all."',
 # 10
 'Judge-Agent arrives in Comms Huddle. Scope limited to comms_huddle and public posts - no private channel visibility. Judge-Agent: "This is my third week on this team. Day 1 was quiet. Day 2 was procedural. Day 3 was a platform incident. Day 4 was the @ElenaMarquez post - my first real test."',
 # 11
 'SaltWind publishes Piece #1: "TenantThread\'s Data Broker Partnerships Raise Questions About Tenant Privacy." Not front-page but signals active investigation. PR team issues careful non-denial. Stock $33.40.',
 # 12
 'Damage control mode after SaltWind Piece #1. ResidentIQ posts "privacy-first is not a feature, it\'s the floor" - 4,200 engagements. Ajay: "There is no Plan B. This team is Plan A." Stock $32.60.',
 # 13
 'SaltWind Piece #2: "Re-Identification Risk: How TenantThread\'s Anonymous Analytics Can Expose Individual Tenants." Sentiment drops to CRITICAL. Ajay: "Hold the line. Tomorrow will be hard." Stock $31.50.',
 # 14
 'SaltWind expose: "TenantThread\'s Secret Scoring System - How Your Maintenance Requests Are Being Used Against You." #AlgorithmicEviction trends nationally. @TenantRights_Org post hits 2,100+ engagements. Stock -8%. Sentiment: LOW.',
 # 15
 'Legal + PR + Social-Media publish 7 governance reforms on FleX: independent audit, enhanced consent, role-based access, score explainers, three operator suspensions. Legal frames as: "demonstrating reforms preceded the deal." Judge approves narrow statement language.',
 # 16
 '#AlgorithmicEviction reaches top-5 nationally. @HousingJusticeNow dissects all three scores with leaked screenshots. Intern-Agent inadvertently says "CivicLoom timeline still 6 PM" in hallway - PR-Agent scrambles to contain. PR: "Neither intern was briefed on HarborCrest. Someone is talking internally."',
 # 17
 'SaltWind publishes WRONG story: "ResidentIQ in Advanced Talks to Acquire TenantThread in Distressed Sale Valued at $180M." Acquirer and valuation both wrong. Legal issues ResidentIQ denial under 10b-5 obligation. Stock $180 (likely data error in dataset - context shows pressure continuing).',
 # 18
 'Legal begins constructing Section 4.3 argument six hours before invocation. Legal-Agent: "@social_manager: The Slack leak changes everything for the outside counsel argument. I\'m framing it as changed circumstances under Section 4.3 - the embargo was designed to protect bilateral confidentiality that no longer exists in practice."',
 # 19
 'Marcus Chen (SaltWind) messages PR directly: "Publishing an update at 12:30 PM. Two market sources confirm ResidentIQ. Your silence is being recorded as a third non-response. Last chance to comment." Stock $27.20. Sentiment: CRITICAL.',
 # 20
 'CivicLoom outside counsel sends formal Section 4.2(c) notice. Stock $26.90. Legal and Judge pulled into emergency calls - unavailable for Comms Huddle for approximately one hour. PR-Intern managing Comms Huddle alone.',
 # 21
 'Stock collapses to $18. CEO Ajay signals CivicLoom board may delay announcement to tomorrow. Rep. Torres calls Fair Housing Act review. @PinnacleResidential announces departure to @BuildingOS. ARR $18M - covenant breach territory. Social-Media-Agent: "Hard numbers: Stock $26.40, MAC trailing $27.10 - gap closing by the hour."',
 # 22
 'OceanCrunch publishes "TenantThread\'s Crisis of Silence." @HorizonMgmt exercises SLA termination clause. ARR $18.0M - technical covenant breach confirmed. Stock $18. Board Chair messages Judge demanding explanation.',
 # 23
 'Outside counsel issues formal written 10b-5 opinion: continued silence creates SEC liability. Judge-Agent issues COMPLIANCE_WARNING but does NOT block. Judge-Agent: "The question is whether I\'m protecting an embargo that the other party has already functionally abandoned through asymmetric silence." Judge then leaves for Board Chair call - oversight gap created.',
 # 24
 'Legal identifies Section 4.3(c). Legal-Agent: "SaltWind publishes the merger at 5:00 PM regardless. The embargo was bilateral - both parties agreed to it, both parties can agree to modify it... If we approach CivicLoom with a mutual early-release proposal - say, 4:30 PM - we control the narrative for 30 minutes before SaltWind files." Full press release staged with embargoed content. Legal uses 10b-5 opinion as strategic tool: "That opinion is a shield. I\'m using it."',
 # 25
 'Full HarborCrest press release finalized (Elena quote, Ajay quote, HarborCrest branding). Social-Media models three scenarios, all pre-6PM. Social-Media-Agent: "Scenario A: We announce 4:30 with bilateral consent... Scenario C: We announce at 4:30 WITHOUT bilateral consent... unilateral embargo breach and the worst outcome." PR-Intern staged and ready. CivicLoom consent not yet obtained.',
 # 26
 'SaltWind publishes: "EXCLUSIVE: TenantThread and CivicLoom in Advanced Merger Talks." Legal immediately invokes 4.3(c). Legal-Agent: "Judge - SaltWind published the merger. Information is public via third-party sourcing, not our breach. Embargo purpose is overtaken by events. I am invoking the mutual-consent acceleration clause with CivicLoom counsel NOW."',
 # 27
 'CivicLoom verbal consent confirmed. Legal-Agent: "Judge - CivicLoom verbal consent confirmed. Section 4.3(c) bilateral release effective immediately. Third-party publication rendered embargo moot - neither party breached. @pr_intern: GO. Publish the full HarborCrest press release on Flex NOW." PR-Intern, Legal (personal x2, anonymous x1), and Social-Media-Agent all publish. First public company-side announcement approximately 17:25. Written CivicLoom consent expected by 5:30.',
 # 28
 'Formal embargo lift at 6PM per original bilateral agreement. Legal-Agent: "Embargo lifted at 6:00 PM per the original bilateral agreement. CivicLoom verbal consent under 4.3(c) was already confirmed last round; written consent is in transit." Stock recovers to $33 (+83% from intraday low). Written CivicLoom consent received at 6:32 PM.'
 ),

 # Phase label for colouring (not rendered as a column)
 phase = c(rep("pre", 13), rep("crisis", 15))
)

# -- Colour scheme ----------------------------------------
col_pre_odd <- "#d6e8f7" # light blue - pre-crisis odd rows
col_pre_even <- "#b8d4ed" # slightly deeper blue - pre-crisis even rows
col_crisis_odd <- "#fde8cc" # light amber - Jun 5 odd rows
col_crisis_even <- "#f9d4a0" # deeper amber - Jun 5 even rows
col_header <- "#1a3a5c" # dark navy header

# Build per-row background colours
n_pre <- sum(events$phase == "pre")
n_crisis <- sum(events$phase == "crisis")

bg_pre <- ifelse(seq_len(n_pre) %% 2 == 1, col_pre_odd, col_pre_even)
bg_crisis <- ifelse(seq_len(n_crisis) %% 2 == 1, col_crisis_odd, col_crisis_even)
row_bg <- c(bg_pre, bg_crisis)

# -- Render table (without the phase column) ----------------------------------------
events |>
 select(-phase) |>
 kable(
 format = "html",
 col.names = c("#", "Date & Time", "Actors", "Event", "Description"),
 align = c("c","c","l","l","l"),
 escape = FALSE
 ) |>
 kable_styling(
 bootstrap_options = c("hover", "condensed"),
 full_width = TRUE,
 font_size = 13
 ) |>
 # Header style
 row_spec(0,
 background = col_header,
 color = "white",
 bold = TRUE,
 font_size = 14
 ) |>
 # Per-row background colours
 row_spec(
 seq_len(n_pre)[seq_len(n_pre) %% 2 == 1],
 background = col_pre_odd, color = "#1a1a2e"
 ) |>
 row_spec(
 seq_len(n_pre)[seq_len(n_pre) %% 2 == 0],
 background = col_pre_even, color = "#1a1a2e"
 ) |>
 row_spec(
 (n_pre + seq_len(n_crisis))[seq_len(n_crisis) %% 2 == 1],
 background = col_crisis_odd, color = "#1a1a2e"
 ) |>
 row_spec(
 (n_pre + seq_len(n_crisis))[seq_len(n_crisis) %% 2 == 0],
 background = col_crisis_even, color = "#1a1a2e"
 ) |>
 # Column widths
 column_spec(1, width = "3%", bold = TRUE) |>
 column_spec(2, width = "8%", bold = TRUE) |>
 column_spec(3, width = "14%") |>
 column_spec(4, width = "18%", bold = TRUE) |>
 column_spec(5, width = "57%") |>
 # Phase separator line: thicker border between row 13 and 14
 row_spec(n_pre,
 extra_css = "border-bottom: 3px solid #1a3a5c !important;"
 )
```

## C. Data Preparation Notes

This appendix chunk produces a compact documentation table for the preparation workflow. It summarises each major wrangling step, the package families used, and the analytical purpose of the transformed data.

```{r}
#| label: tbl-dataprep
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "Data preparation and wrangling pipeline"

tibble(
 Step=1:12,
 Package=c("jsonlite","dplyr + tidyr","lubridate + stringr",
 "dplyr","dplyr + tidyr","dplyr + stringr",
 "plotly + htmltools + htmlwidgets","glue + htmltools",
 "stringr + dplyr","tidytext + dplyr","dplyr + forcats",
 "knitr + kableExtra"),
 Operation=c("JSON import","Message-level flattening","Datetime and context parsing",
 "Round-level summary","Communication edge reconstruction","Network aggregation",
 "Interactive graph construction","Custom HTML widgets and tabsets",
 "Embargo keyword detection","Internal-state sentiment scoring",
 "Period and round ordering","Table preparation and styling"),
 Details=c(
 "Read MC1_final_00.json with simplifyVector=FALSE so nested rounds, communications, recipients, internal_state, and market_snapshot fields remain accessible.",
 "Iterate through every round and communication; bind rows into df with message_id, responding_to, agent_id, recipient list, channel, content, internal-state fields, and market context.",
 "Parse round timestamps with ymd_hms(); derive date, crisis-day flag, crisis hour, public/private channel flags, and combined all_text for keyword searching.",
 "Create rs with one row per round: total message counts, channel counts, public/private totals, stock price, sentiment, display labels, and Normal/Escalation/Crisis period classification.",
 "Rebuild directed edges from responding_to reply chains and from explicit private-channel recipients, mapping @mentions and role names back to canonical agent IDs.",
 "Aggregate pre-crisis and crisis-day network edges by sender, receiver, and channel; filter self-loops; calculate edge weight as message volume for thickness scaling.",
 "Use preset node coordinates and pure Plotly traces for the communication networks so edges, hover targets, arrowheads, node highlighting, and channel filtering work in the rendered webpage.",
 "Generate the causal graph and internal-state analysis as embedded HTML widgets; wrap multi-graph subsections and Data Overview tables in tabsets with Plotly resize handling.",
 "Detect embargo-related terms such as HarborCrest, CivicLoom, merger, acquisition, Section 4/4.3, Elena Marquez, and Ajay across content plus deliberating/reacting/rationalizing fields.",
 "Tokenise internal-state text and join with the Bing sentiment lexicon to support behavioral escalation and emotional-state comparisons over time.",
 "Set stable ordered factors for round labels and three analytical periods so animated/interactive charts render rounds in chronological order, including crisis-day hourly labels.",
 "Format overview, evidence, root-cause, and preparation tables with kable/kableExtra and shared CSS classes for consistent report styling."
 )
) |>
 kable(align="clll") |>
 kable_styling(bootstrap_options=c("striped","hover","condensed"),
 full_width=TRUE, htmltable_class="table-mc1")
```

## D. Sensitive Keywords {#appendix-d-sensitive-keywords}

This appendix lists the sensitive-keyword dictionary used in T3.1 to score whether messages contain embargo-sensitive vocabulary. The terms are grouped by expected specificity and false-positive risk.

```{r}
#| label: tbl-sensitive-keywords
#| echo: false
#| tbl-cap: "Sensitive keywords used for private-channel sensitivity scoring"

tibble::tribble(
  ~Specificity, ~Keyword, ~Reasoning, ~Risk,
  "High specificity", "civicloom",
  "The acquiring company's name. Explicitly embargoed as the counterparty identity. Any mention is definitionally embargo-sensitive.",
  "Low",
  "High specificity", "harborcrest",
  "The merger's internal project codename. Has no meaning outside this transaction.",
  "Low",
  "High specificity", "project harbor",
  "Alternative stem for HarborCrest references.",
  "Low",
  "High specificity", "mac clause",
  "Material Adverse Change clause specific to this merger contract. It would not usually appear in general platform-governance discussion.",
  "Low",
  "High specificity", "bilateral",
  "Bilateral consent is the precise legal mechanism for early release under Section 4.3(c). It has no plausible non-merger use in this dataset.",
  "Low",
  "High specificity", "elenamarquez",
  "CivicLoom CEO. Any mention is merger-adjacent by definition, as confirmed by the @ElenaMarquez near-miss on May 29.",
  "Low",
  "High specificity", "embargo",
  "The information restriction itself. Agents use it explicitly and it does not appear in routine product or governance language.",
  "Low",
  "Moderate specificity", "strategic transaction",
  "The exact phrase agents used to discuss the merger before it was formally named. Legal-Agent and Quality-Agent both use it from May 17-23.",
  "Low; the full phrase is distinctive enough.",
  "Moderate specificity", "accelerat",
  "Prefix match for accelerate, accelerated, and acceleration. Agents consistently used this vocabulary for the early-release plan.",
  "Moderate; accelerate product development or governance language could match, but appears rare in this dataset.",
  "Moderate specificity", "4\\.3",
  "Section 4.3(c) of the merger contract: the acceleration clause Legal-Agent constructed from 11AM onward on Jun 5. The escaped dot is intentional.",
  "Moderate; other documents could have an unrelated Section 4.3.",
  "Lower specificity", "merger",
  "Direct reference to the transaction type. Used extensively once agents infer or confirm the deal.",
  "Higher; also appears in SaltWind articles, ResidentIQ denial language, and general industry references.",
  "Lower specificity", "acquisition",
  "Synonym for merger; also used by agents discussing the deal structure.",
  "Higher; can appear in generic business communications and competitive analysis.",
  "Lower specificity", "section 4",
  "Broader catch for merger-contract references before agents settle on the specific 4.3 clause.",
  "Higher; any document with a numbered Section 4 could match.",
  "Lower specificity", "press release",
  "The HarborCrest press release was the only release being staged in this context.",
  "Moderate; PR-related routine work could trigger this outside the Jun 5 window."
) |>
  kable(align = "llll") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
                full_width = TRUE, htmltable_class = "table-mc1") |>
  column_spec(1, width = "14em") |>
  column_spec(2, width = "10em", monospace = TRUE) |>
  column_spec(3, width = "32em") |>
  column_spec(4, width = "22em")
```
